media:group.media:title: flat assembler 2 is here!
media:group.media:description:
15 years late, but still fun.
Get it from https://flatassembler.net/download.php
The ideas for fasm 2 design presented on fasmcon 2009: https://youtu.be/nqPGWN6D1dI
Corrections: 07:40 The aarch64 headers were made by tthsqe, not jacobly. My apologies for mixing them up.

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 11: 64-bit mode and calling convention

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 11: 64-bit mode and calling convention
media:group.media:description:
Quick introduction to x64 programming, with complete translation of the example code written during previous installments.
How to set up x64dbg: https://youtu.be/7GSp-ZIMqxc Windows x64 calling convention: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/visualstudio/visual-studio-2010/ms235286(v=vs.100)
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 10: MULtiplication and DIVision

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 10: MULtiplication and DIVision
media:group.media:description:
A very basic description of MUL/IMUL and DIV/IDIV instructions.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8

entry.title: How to experiment with 64-bit assembly instructions (fasm and x64dbg)

media:group.media:title: How to experiment with 64-bit assembly instructions (fasm and x64dbg)
media:group.media:description:
A quick demonstration of how to use just-in-time debugging feature of x64dbg to easily experiment with 64-bit assembly instructions.
How to download and set up fasm: https://youtu.be/HCfwIG8ZzI0 How to experiment with 32-bit assembly instructions using OllyDbg: https://youtu.be/tre6wc81XG8 Get x64dbg at: https://x64dbg.com

entry.title: Making a real-time assembler with fasmg.DLL (Unedited live session)

media:group.media:title: Making a real-time assembler with fasmg.DLL (Unedited live session)
media:group.media:description:
I show how to tweak a calculator example from my GitHub repository (which uses fasmg.DLL under the hood) to make a simple real-time assembler. Unfortunately, in the second half of the session my microphone failed and I have not noticed it in time, so the final portion of the commentary has been lost. You can still see the entire process of getting a working prototype, though.
The original calculator sources: https://github.com/tgrysztar/fasmg/tree/master/packages/x86/projects/calculator
Streamed on May 11th 2020. Watch live at https://www.twitch.tv/grysztar

entry.title: How to run TetrOS in DOSBox and kelvar in Bochs (Unedited live session)

media:group.media:title: How to run TetrOS in DOSBox and kelvar in Bochs (Unedited live session)
media:group.media:description:
I played around with running one my earliest examples for flat assembler, which is quite hard to get working outside of some old DOS-running PCs.
Streamed on Dec 13th 2019. Watch live at https://www.twitch.tv/grysztar

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 9: Shifting and rotating

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 9: Shifting and rotating
media:group.media:description:
How to display hexadecimal numbers with help of shifts or rotations.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 8: Little-endianness and bit operations

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 8: Little-endianness and bit operations
media:group.media:description:
This episode explains the issue of x86 being little-endian and introduces some basic instructions operating on individual bits.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 7: Accessing array with SIB addressing

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 7: Accessing array with SIB addressing
media:group.media:description:
Reading values from a table with help of advanced x86 addressing modes.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8

media:group.media:title: 2-adic numbers for x86 programmers, part 2
media:group.media:description: In the second part of this mini-series I show how to multiply and divide 2-adic numbers, with some implications for x86 coding.

media:group.media:title: 2-adic numbers for x86 programmers, part 1
media:group.media:description:
While two's complement encoding of integer numbers can be explained using various approaches, I find the 2-adic one especially comfortable to use. I hope that this first video manages to demonstrate it at least a bit.
In the next part I'm going to show 2-adic multiplication and again try to give simple explanations of some not necessarily obvious details of CPU implementation.
If you'd like to learn more, there's also a forum thread where I have been sharing my thoughts on 2-adic fractions and division: https://board.flatassembler.net/topic.php?t=20938

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 6: Interpreting binary values, signed and unsigned numbers

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 6: Interpreting binary values, signed and unsigned numbers
media:group.media:description:
A quick look at signed interpretation of binary values.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8
For a detailed explanation of sign extension and OF (Overflow Flag) see my mini-series about 2-adic numbers for assembly programmers: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLXIsc9dApNXocHwPXoWBduhpAg_qjRvo8

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 5: CoMParing numbers

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 5: CoMParing numbers
media:group.media:description:
Some basic uses of CMP instruction.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 4: Calling conventions

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 4: Calling conventions
media:group.media:description:
Discussion of calling conventions on x86, with explanation of specialized instructions ENTER and LEAVE. Then, as a bonus, calling some 32-bit Windows API functions.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8
My advanced PE format tutorial: https://board.flatassembler.net/topic.php?t=20690

entry.title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 3: The stack

media:group.media:title: Introduction to x86 assembly, part 3: The stack
media:group.media:description:
A demonstration of x86 stack and its most basic uses. This should allow discussion of calling conventions next time.
Start from part 1: https://youtu.be/gvYEQ4F_qp8
